title: camassia esculenta
intro: Camassia esculenta, also known as camas or wild hyacinth, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its blue or white bell-shaped flowers and edible bulbs. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions. The bulbs of Camassia esculenta are rich in inulin, a type of prebiotic fiber that can help lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and support the growth of beneficial gut bacteria. Camassia esculenta can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is also used as a food source, often roasted or dried as a sweet-tasting root vegetable.
